newsSinceJun6: No material company-specific news since June 6. Stock printed a fresh 52-week high on June 9 (intraday ~$81.7) on a +2.27% day, extending a +65.5% YTD / ~+110% one-year run. Coverage is momentum-flavored ('is it too late after a 71% rally'), with only minor analyst-rating drift (Weiss B->B+ on May 28). Last fundamental datapoint remains the April 23 Q1 2026 record results (sales $176.6M, +7.7%; record net income $17.8M); no new contracts, design wins, M&A, or capacity announcements found.

sources.label: Markets Daily - Gorman-Rupp hits new 12-month high (Jun 9, 2026)
Yahoo Finance - Is It Too Late To Consider Gorman-Rupp After A 71% One-Year Rally?

verdicts.rankRationale: This page rewards hidden, under-owned latency, and GRC is being discovered in real time: fresh 52-week high today, +65.5% YTD, and mainstream 'too late to buy?' coverage, all with zero new fundamental evidence since June 6. Run-up without new evidence reduces residual alpha on a crowding-sensitive list, so the name slips despite an intact thesis.
verdicts.bull: AI data-center liquid-cooling and water-handling demand is starting to attach to industrial pump names, and GRC's municipal/industrial installed base plus record Q1 earnings give it a credible on-ramp before any explicit DC revenue is disclosed.
verdicts.neutral: The thesis is unchanged and unproven in equal measure: no disclosed data-center wins, but no disconfirming news either; the stock is simply rerating ahead of evidence.
verdicts.bear: At ~+110% over one year and a $2B cap, the 'quiet industrial' framing is dead; if data-center pump revenue never materializes, GRC is a fully-priced municipal pump company with mean-reversion risk on the AI premium.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Explicit data-center / cooling-loop order disclosure or segment commentary at Q2 2026 earnings (late July).
verdicts.primaryRisk: Momentum unwind: the YTD rerating reverses if AI-cooling attachment fails to show up in orders or backlog.

verdicts.rankRationale: The strict screen is pathway-evidence weighted, and the evidence base (industrial/municipal pump expertise migrating into CDU loops, water reuse, facility fluid systems; GRI's server/electronics cooling line) is unchanged since yesterday's hold. Only price has moved (+2.27%, 52-week high), which trims asymmetry slightly but does not alter the pathway, so hold with minor drift.
verdicts.bull: GRI Pumps already lists server and electronics cooling as an application area, so the strict pathway from pump expertise to CDU/facility water loops is real and could surface in backlog before consensus models it.
verdicts.neutral: No new pathway evidence since the April Q1 print; high evidence quality and medium AI-chain risk both stand, with valuation now doing more of the work.
verdicts.bear: CDU and high-end liquid-cooling pumps are a specialized, competitive niche (incumbent thermal players, Asian OEMs); GRC's municipal-heavy mix may never convert the adjacency at a scale that matters to a $2B cap up 65% YTD.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: First named CDU/data-center fluid-system win or backlog disclosure tying orders to data-center construction.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Adjacency never converts: AI-cooling demand routes to specialist thermal vendors, leaving GRC's rerating unsupported.
